audit_method: Full-batch STRICT code-validity pass (all 130 records) + corpus re-read for all 5 medium-confidence records + spot-check corpus re-read of 30 high-confidence records + targeted pattern hunts across relevant subsets: MED/prefet-escalade wording check on all 15 records tagged actor=A03_PREFET or stage=S05_ESCALADE; 'obs' vs 'écart' label check on all 6 records using that site convention; 'précédente inspection' wording check on all T1-tagged records; False-M08 keyword scan on all 42 M08_CONFORMITE records.

Hunter's own reasoning states "Pas de MED pour le moment" and the corpus explicitly reads "l'inspection ne propose pas, pour le moment de mise en demeure" — M04_MISE_EN_DEMEURE contradicts the source text.

Finding concerns two auxiliary staff lacking training attestations ("n'ont pas formellement suivi la formation"), matching M01_DOCUMENTATION's 'attestations' signal, not a physical/technical NC (M14). The inspection also only 'suggère' rather than formally demands.

Corpus labels this finding 'obs :' (observation) not 'écart :' (NC) — same convention as sibling record f13 in this batch, which Hunter correctly tagged G2. Incertitude on system sizing demonstration is documentary/formal, not a confirmed physical NC.

Exploitant does not claim ignorance of the obligation; he argues on technical grounds that green wood produces no dust. The inspection explicitly refutes this with a counter-technical argument (norm citation) — a contested diagnostic (R10_TENSION), not méconnaissance (R04).

Periodic groundwater analysis (M07_CONTROLE) functioning correctly per prescriptions detected a concentration anomaly the report itself attributes most likely to external malveillance, not an installation-level physical/technical NC (M14) by the exploitant.
A formal 'Demande à formuler' exists (continued surveillance attention required), which is an injunction (S02) rather than a pure observation (S01).
Corpus contains no mention of a mise en demeure or préfet escalation — the inspection directly instructs the exploitant to regularize the cessation procedure.
No MED/escalade in text; this is a formal injunction (S02), not S05_ESCALADE.
Corpus contains no mise en demeure or préfet escalation — the inspection itself asks the exploitant to regularize within 3 months (enregistrement or cessation).
No MED/escalade in text; this is a formal injunction (S02), not S05_ESCALADE.
Text explicitly: "M. GERMAIN s'est engagé à aider M. DESPRES à résorber la décharge" — an explicit future commitment, matching R02_PROMESSE, not the neutral default R14.
New waste ('déchets supplémentaires... ont été ajoutés') on top of the still-unresolved prior dump matches T6_ACCUMULATION's signal ('s'ajoute') more precisely than general T5_AGGRAVATION.

false_positive_rate_note: Computed on substantive classification errors only (8 of 130 records, 6.2%), excluding the systematic STRICT-formatting fix (m_DELAI misplaced in `mechanisms` instead of `modifiers`, affecting 46 records, plus one stray R04 code in `mechanisms`) which is a mechanical rule violation rather than a classification judgment error. Including the mechanical fixes, 53/130 records (40.8%) were changed in some field.
error_patterns: 1) Systematic STRICT violation: Hunter placed the m_DELAI modifier inside the `mechanisms` array instead of `modifiers` in 46/130 records (35%) — a mechanical, consistent mistake, not a judgment error. One record (garage-costantini...f01) also had the dynamic-axis code R04 stray inside `mechanisms`. 2) MED/escalade over-attribution: 2 records (gery-philippe...f01, gb-autos...f01) were tagged actor=A03_PREFET / stage=S05_ESCALADE with no mise-en-demeure or préfet mention anywhere in the corpus — Hunter appears to pattern-match 'activité illégale' + G4 to an escalade stage even when the text describes a plain inspection injunction. One further record (garage-valeix...f01) kept M04_MISE_EN_DEMEURE in `mechanisms` despite its own reasoning field stating 'Pas de MED pour le moment', and despite the tag correctly using S02_INJONCTION. 3) M14 over-extension: 2 records mislabeled a documentary/procedural finding (missing training attestations; a lab-detected groundwater anomaly attributed to probable external malveillance) as M14_CONSTAT_TECHNIQUE (physical/technical NC) rather than M01_DOCUMENTATION or M07_CONTROLE. 4) R02/R04 default drift: one record (garbaye...f05) tagged R04_MECONNAISSANCE for a contested technical argument (diagnostic dispute, not ignorance of the obligation) — should be R10_TENSION; another (germain-steve...2025f02) used the neutral default R14 despite an explicit future commitment ('s'est engagé à aider...') that matches R02_PROMESSE, with trajectory T5 where the accumulation signal (T6) fit better. 5) Gravity: one record (gaia...f12) was tagged G3 despite the site's own report using the milder 'obs :' label (vs 'écart :' used elsewhere in the same corpus for confirmed NCs) — corrected to G2 to match its sibling record already tagged G2 under the same convention.
verdict: Hunter's substantive classification quality is good: 8/130 records (6.2%) needed a judgment correction, well under the <15% target, M15_AUTRE usage is 0%, and the 3 R02_PROMESSE and ~13 M17_CLASSIFICATION assignments checked were all well-supported by explicit textual signals. The dominant issue in this batch is purely mechanical: Hunter consistently placed the m_DELAI modifier inside `mechanisms` instead of `modifiers` (46/130 records), which would have failed STRICT validation on every affected record if left uncorrected. final-35.json fixes all of these plus the 8 substantive errors and passes scripts/tag_status.py with zero validation errors.
